mes generally unfortunate, indicates many
sorrows, disappointments, poverty and an uphill road
generally. By using considerable discretion-qualifying or
moderating your deductions to conform with the
astrological testimonies—you can unfold your powers of
prediction. Summing up, the above remarks:— (a) consider
the nature of the rising planet, (b) whether it is in debility or
exaltation, in a friendly or inimical sign, (c) note the aspects
it receives from the plariets—the houses from which
aspects are cast and the conjunctions the rising planet has,
(d) the nature of the sign—movable,

Concerning the First House 41
immovable, common, airy, watery etc. Finally, blend all the
influences carefully.
Sun in the First House.—Strong moral nature,
righteous-minded, ambition and love of power, tends to be
well supported by good health and vitality. Cheerfulness
and an optimistic temperament help to ensure popularity. It
adds respect to the personality and gives lofty motives. If
